posts.author: Alo11 (Adin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Is there a way to template a group (not a board), so that when I add new groups to my existing board they come preloaded with whatever I need

What are you trying to achieve

Streamline workflow – right now, I’m duplicating boards in a slow and clumsy process

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Adin,

At the moment, Monday does not support creating a reusable Group Template that you can insert into a board with preset columns, automations, items, or structure. But you can Create a separate Template Board with your pre-made groups and duplicate those groups into your active board when needed.

This gives you a manual but clean and consistent group template system

Dr. Tanvi Sachar

Monday Certified Partner, Monday Wizard

posts.author: andrewp (Andrzej P)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @adinap,

Our app Advanced Templates can do exactly what you’re describing. It lets you create templates for groups, items, and subitems, so you can quickly add new groups preloaded with everything you need — either manually or automatically.

You can also set dynamic due dates relative to the item’s creation date, making the workflow even smoother.

Cheers,
Andrew
APMD SOFT

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Alo11 ,

Are you trying to template a group as is per board, or are you trying to create a group that’ll work on any and every board?

Template Group for Its Board

This scenario indicates that you have a specific group with already made items and other column data you automatically want added to the board given the situation.

In my company, we have a Yearly Budget board. Each group is a specific year. I have a template group that defines the row item name and any specific data. I have a workflows automation that triggers every year to duplicate the template group as a new group for the following year. The workflows automation grants the group’s name as the following year. So, on January 1, 2026, a new group will be created named 2027. I also use the free app “Workflow Blocks: Formula” to help me with this.

You can do this for every board you have, but the group is specific to the board.

Template Group for All Boards

Create a separate board that is a template board. You do NOT have to set it as a standard or managed template. In fact, it is preferable that you don’t do that.

When it is needed, you can duplicate the group and then move the group to another board. Depending on your situation, you can probably set some sort of workflows or automations to automate this flow.

Feel free to reach out if you have any specific questions. I’m interested to know your process and if automations could help with this.
